      "notes": "Zoom toward mouse position for natural feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F004",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Double-click zoom",
      "steps": [
        "Double-click zooms in 2x at click location",
        "Works at any current zoom level (respects max)",
        "Animation is smooth (no jarring jump)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Quick zoom in gesture"
    },
    {
      "id": "F005",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom button controls",
      "steps": [
        "Plus button zooms in by 1.5x",
        "Minus button zooms out by 1.5x",
        "Reset button returns to zoom 1.0 and pan origin",
        "Buttons zoom toward center of viewport"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Alternative for users without scroll wheel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F006",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Macro layer renders at zoom < 0.5x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom out below 0.5x to trigger Macro view",
        "Heatmap displays with Yellow-Orange-Red gradient",
        "Cell density reflects task count per month/train",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MACRO' with yellow badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Strategic 10-20 year overview"
    },
    {
      "id": "F007",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Meso layer renders at zoom 0.5x-2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom to 0.5x-2x range to trigger Meso view",
        "Task blocks render as colored rectangles (DNA strips)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MESO' with blue badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Tactical 2-5 year view with task blocks"
    },
    {
      "id": "F008",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Micro layer renders at zoom > 2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom above 2x to trigger Micro view",
        "Task cards render with shadows and rounded corners",
        "Grid lines show days (vertical) and trains (horizontal)",
        "Task type labels visible when card is wide enough",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MICRO' with green badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Operational 4-8 week view with detailed cards"
    },
    {
      "id": "F009",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "LOD transitions are smooth",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom from Macro to Meso - no flickering",
        "Zoom from Meso to Micro - no flickering",
        "State persists correctly across transitions",
        "Performance stays smooth during transition"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F006",
      "notes": "Critical for Google Earth-like UX feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F010",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Sidebar displays train IDs synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Train IDs visible in left sidebar",
        "IDs update as canvas pans vertically",
        "Row height matches canvas zoom level",
        "Only visible trains are rendered (virtual scroll)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Sidebar syncs with panY position"
    },
    {
      "id": "F011",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Timeline header displays dates synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Timeline labels update as canvas pans horizontally",
        "Granularity changes based on zoom (years/months/weeks/days)",
        "Labels are readable and don't overlap",
        "Timeline syncs with panX position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Adaptive granularity: years at low zoom, days at high zoom"
    },
    {
      "id": "F012",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Conflict indicators visible on tasks",
      "steps": [
        "Tasks with conflicts show red border/glow",
        "Conflicts visible in Meso layer (red stroke)",
        "Conflicts visible in Micro layer (red glow shadow)",
        "~5% of tasks have conflict flag for testing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Red highlight draws attention to scheduling issues"
    },
    {
      "id": "F013",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Task colors match design specifications",
      "steps": [
        "Maintenance tasks: Blue gradient (#3b82f6 to #2563eb)",
        "Overhaul tasks: Orange gradient (#f59e0b to #d97706)",
        "Inspection tasks: Gray gradient (#9ca3af to #6b7280)",
        "Gradients render correctly at all zoom levels"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Color coding matches docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md"
    },
    {
      "id": "F014",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "Onboarding instructions display and auto-hide",
      "steps": [
        "Instructions tooltip visible on page load",
        "Shows: 'Scroll to zoom - Drag to pan - Double-click to zoom in'",
        "Auto-fades after 5 seconds",
        "Non-intrusive (pointer-events: none)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Progressive disclosure - teach on first use"
    },
    {
      "id": "F015",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "LOD legend explains zoom levels",
      "steps": [
        "Legend panel visible in bottom-left corner",
        "Shows Macro (yellow), Meso (blue), Micro (green) colors",
        "Describes zoom thresholds (<0.5x, 0.5-2x, >2x)",
        "Helps users understand semantic zoom concept"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Educational UI element"
    },
    {
      "id": "F101",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Create Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS app skeleton",
      "steps": [
        "Create apps/canvas directory with Vite config",
        "Install React 18.3.1, BlueprintJS 6.1.0",
        "Create basic App.tsx with BlueprintJS Navbar",
        "App runs on localhost:5173 without errors",
        "BlueprintJS styles load correctly"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "Foundation for production app - React 18 required for BlueprintJS 6"
    },
    {
      "id": "F102",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Add R3F Canvas with OrthographicCamera",
      "steps": [
        "Install @react-three/fiber and @react-three/drei",
        "Create InfiniteCanvas component with R3F Canvas",
        "Setup OrthographicCamera for 2D-like view",
        "Canvas fills available space below navbar",
        "No WebGL errors in console"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "R3F 8.x compatible with React 18. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F103",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Basic BlueprintJS layout (Navbar, sidebar placeholder)",
      "steps": [
        "Navbar with app title and zoom indicator",
        "Left sidebar container for train IDs",
        "Main area for R3F canvas",
        "Layout uses flexbox, fills viewport",
        "Responsive to window resize"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "BlueprintJS Card, Navbar, ButtonGroup. Sidebar syncs with canvas panY. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F104",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Data types and mock generator",
      "steps": [
        "Create Train, Task, Config TypeScript interfaces",
        "Implement generateData() function",
        "Generate 50 trains with 10 years of tasks",
        "~5% tasks have conflict flag",
        "Data available via React context or store"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "types.ts + generator.ts in apps/canvas/src/data/. Persistent types use ISO strings, render types use numeric offsets."
    },
    {
      "id": "F105",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MesoLayer - InstancedMesh task blocks",
      "steps": [
        "Create MesoLayer component using InstancedMesh",
        "Render task blocks as colored rectangles",
        "Colors: blue (maintenance), orange (overhaul), gray (inspection)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "Tasks visible at default zoom (1.0x)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F104",
      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh. Fixed architectural issue: camera init now uses R3F size (not container div). Tasks aligned with sidebar. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F106",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Pan and Zoom camera controls",
      "steps": [
        "Drag to pan (mousedown + mousemove)",
        "Scroll to zoom (wheel event)",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor",
        "Respect MIN_ZOOM (0.1) and MAX_ZOOM (5.0)",
        "Cursor changes to grab/grabbing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "Pan bounded to world limits. Zoom via wheel and buttons. Camera initialized using R3F size (not container div). Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F107",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "LOD state management",
      "steps": [
        "Create useViewport hook with zoom, panX, panY state",
        "Compute LOD from zoom: MACRO (<0.5), MESO (0.5-2), MICRO (>2)",
        "LOD changes trigger layer visibility",
        "State persists across renders",
        "Export state for UI components"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "Implemented in App.tsx (getLOD, zoom/panY state). Hook extraction optional. Layer visibility tested when F105+ exist."
    },
    {
      "id": "F108",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MacroLayer - Heatmap cells",
      "steps": [
        "Create MacroLayer component",
        "Render heatmap cells based on task density",
        "Color gradient: yellow -> orange -> red",
        "Only visible when zoom < 0.5x",
        "Smooth transition from MesoLayer"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F105",
      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh. Task density aggregated by month. Color gradient: light yellow -> amber -> orange -> red. Year markers included. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F109",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MicroLayer - Html task cards with labels",
      "steps": [
        "Create MicroLayer using drei Html component",
        "Render detailed task cards with shadows",
        "Show task type label when card wide enough",
        "Grid lines for days and trains",
        "Only visible when zoom > 2x"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F105",
      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh for task cards + drei Html for labels. Includes grid lines, row backgrounds, and conflict glow effects. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F110",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Smooth LOD transitions",
      "steps": [
        "Transition between layers without flickering",
        "No visual artifacts during zoom",
        "State persists across LOD changes",
        "Performance stays smooth"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F108",
      "notes": "Implemented with blend zones (+/-0.15) around LOD thresholds. Adjacent layers render simultaneously with interpolated opacity during transitions. All layers support opacity prop. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F111",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "TrainSidebar synced with camera Y",
      "steps": [
        "Create TrainSidebar component with BlueprintJS Card",
        "Display train IDs in list",
        "Sync position with camera panY",
        "Row height scales with zoom",
        "Virtual scroll for performance"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F107",
      "notes": "Implemented in App.tsx. Sidebar syncs via sidebarOffset = -panY * zoom. Virtual scroll optional for future."
    },
    {
      "id": "F112",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Timeline header with adaptive granularity",
      "steps": [
        "Create TimelineHeader component",
        "Show date labels synced with panX",
        "Adapt granularity: years -> months -> weeks -> days",
        "Labels don't overlap",
        "Positioned above canvas"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F105",
      "notes": "Implemented with adaptive granularity based on zoom: months (0.5-1.5x), weeks (1.5-3.0x), days (>3.0x). Fixed Vite interface export issue using 'import type'. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F113",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "ZoomControls and LODIndicator in Navbar",
      "steps": [
        "Add zoom level text (e.g., '1.00x') to Navbar",
        "Add LOD indicator Tag (MACRO/MESO/MICRO)",
        "Add ButtonGroup with +/- zoom buttons",
        "Add Reset button",
        "Style with BlueprintJS Intent colors"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F107",
      "notes": "Fully implemented in App.tsx Navbar with BlueprintJS components."
    },
    {
      "id": "F114",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Conflict indicators (Intent.DANGER styling)",
      "steps": [
        "Tasks with conflict flag show red styling",
        "Use BlueprintJS Intent.DANGER color (#cd4246)",
        "Visible in Meso and Micro layers",
        "Red glow/border effect",
        "~5% of tasks show conflict"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F105",
      "notes": "Implemented in MesoLayer with red color (#ef4444) and glow effect via ConflictGlows component. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F115",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Instructions Toaster (auto-dismiss)",
      "steps": [
        "Show Toaster on first load",
        "Message: 'Scroll to zoom - Drag to pan'",
        "Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds",
        "Use BlueprintJS Toaster component",
        "Non-blocking (pointer-events: none)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F103",
      "notes": "Implemented with BlueprintJS OverlayToaster.createAsync(). Shows on load with 5s timeout. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    }
  ],
  "categories": {

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "steps": [
        "Research Playwright headless configuration options",
        "Update MCP settings for headless mode",
        "Test that browser windows don't steal focus during visual tests",
        "Document configuration in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Playwright MCP configured with --headless flag in .mcp.json. Deny permissions added to block access to personal directories."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking enabled",
        "Research QA best practices for canvas/WebGL applications",
        "Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests",
        "Define QA checklist based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries",
        "Update harness hooks to enforce QA step",
        "Document in docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md"
      ],

# MMS System Documentation

This directory contains domain knowledge, requirements, research, and UX specifications for the Metro Integrated Planning System.

## Document Index

| Document | Description | Audience |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| [DOMAIN.md](./DOMAIN.md) | Fleet composition, maintenance types, domain entities | Developers, Domain Experts |
| [REQUIREMENTS.md](./REQUIREMENTS.md) | Business requirements (from RFQ) | Product, Engineering |
| [UX_SPECIFICATION.md](./UX_SPECIFICATION.md) | UX design and interaction patterns | Designers, Frontend Devs |
| [UX_RESEARCH.md](./UX_RESEARCH.md) | Pan/zoom UX research (Figma, Maps, iOS patterns) | Frontend Devs |
| [RESEARCH.md](./RESEARCH.md) | Industry research on maintenance scheduling | All stakeholders |
| [QA_PROTOCOL.md](./QA_PROTOCOL.md) | Mandatory QA testing protocol for canvas/WebGL | AI Agents, QA |

## Project Context

### What is MMS?

The **Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)** is a high-performance visualization tool for railway rolling stock maintenance planning. It provides a "Single Pane of Glass" solution that unifies:

- **Strategic Planning** (1-12 years): Budget allocation, major overhauls
- **Tactical Planning** (weeks-months): Workload balancing, resource leveling
- **Operational Planning** (daily): Run assignments, track allocation

### The "Semantic Zoom" Concept

Inspired by Google Earth, the system implements **semantic zoom** - the visualization automatically transforms based on zoom level:

| Zoom Level | Threshold | Time Scale | Visualization |
|------------|-----------|------------|---------------|
| Macro | < 0.5x | 10-20 years | Heatmap (yellow→orange→red) |
| Meso | 0.5-2x | 2-5 years | Task blocks (DNA pattern) |
| Micro | > 2x | 4-8 weeks | Detailed cards with labels |

### Current Implementation

**Production:** `apps/canvas` - React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6 app with semantic zoom.

```bash
# Run production app (http://localhost:5173)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev
```

**Production Features (F101-F117):**

| Phase | Features | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| **Foundation** | F101-F103 | Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS + R3F setup |
| **Core Rendering** | F104-F107 | Data, MesoLayer, pan/zoom, LOD state |
| **All Layers** | F108-F110 | MacroLayer, MicroLayer, smooth transitions |
| **UI Integration** | F111-F114 | Sidebar, timeline, controls, conflicts |
| **Polish** | F115-F117 | Instructions, performance, visual parity |

**POC Reference:**

The POC (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) serves as visual reference only:
- Visual appearance of semantic zoom UX
- 3 LOD levels (Macro/Meso/Micro)
- Sidebar and timeline layout
- Conflict visual indicators

See `features.json` for complete feature list and status.

## Relationship to Implementation

### Production App (`apps/canvas`)

The UX patterns in [UX_SPECIFICATION.md](./UX_SPECIFICATION.md) are implemented in:
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/` - R3F rendering components
- `apps/canvas/src/components/` - BlueprintJS UI components
- `apps/canvas/src/hooks/` - State management (useViewport)

### Domain Entities

The domain entities in [DOMAIN.md](./DOMAIN.md) are implemented in:
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - TypeScript interfaces
- `apps/canvas/src/data/generator.ts` - Mock data generation

### POC Reference

The validated prototype (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) serves as visual reference only:
- Demonstrates semantic zoom concept visually
- Visual reference for production implementation
- Shows what the 3 LOD levels should look like

### Future Development

The full RFQ requirements in [REQUIREMENTS.md](./REQUIREMENTS.md) include features not yet implemented:
- Real-time mileage data integration
- What-If scenario simulation
- Cost forecasting and budget tracking
- IBM Maximo integration

These represent the product roadmap beyond the current demo.

## Quick Links

- **Technical Architecture**: See `agent_docs/architecture.md`
- **Visual References**: See `design/README.md`
- **Code Conventions**: See `agent_docs/conventions.md`
- **Session Progress**: See `claude-progress.txt`

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)

## Project Overview
High-Performance Enterprise Integrated Planning System with "Semantic Zoom" UX (Google Earth-style) for metro fleet management. Renders 50+ trains and 10 years of tasks at constant 60fps using WebGL.

---

## HARNESS: Fundamental Concept (READ THIS FIRST)

> **This project uses a Harness.** If you don't know what that is, STOP and research:
> - [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

### What is a Harness?

A **Harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.

**Problem:** Each new Claude Code session starts with NO memory of previous sessions.

**Solution:** State files persist context between sessions:
- `claude-progress.txt` → session history
- `features.json` → requirements with status (passes: true/false)

### Work Protocol

```
1. READ claude-progress.txt (understand current state)
2. READ features.json (find next feature)
3. IMPLEMENT one feature
4. TEST visually (Playwright MCP)
5. COMMIT after passing
6. UPDATE claude-progress.txt
7. STOP (leave codebase production-ready)
```

### Self-Correction Protocol

If you don't understand what harness is after reading this:
1. **SEARCH:** WebSearch "Anthropic harness agents 2025"
2. **ANALYZE:** Why was local documentation unclear?
3. **FIX:** Update CLAUDE.md or hooks to improve clarity
4. **DOCUMENT:** Record the fix in claude-progress.txt

### Harness Files

| File                  | Purpose              | When to Read       |
|-----------------------|----------------------|--------------------|
| `claude-progress.txt` | Session history      | **ALWAYS first**   |
| `features.json`       | Features with status | After progress.txt |
| `CLAUDE.md`           | Project rules        | Reference          |

---

## Security & Permissions

### File Access Restrictions

This project uses **deny permissions** to prevent access to personal directories outside the project.

**Protected directories** (access denied):
- `~/Desktop`, `~/Documents`, `~/Downloads`
- `~/Pictures`, `~/Movies`, `~/Music`
- Any `find ~` or `find /Users` commands

**Configuration:** `.claude/settings.local.json` contains deny rules that block:
- Read/Write/Edit/Glob/Grep operations on protected paths
- Bash commands that scan home directories

### Playwright MCP Configuration

Visual testing uses Playwright MCP in **headless mode** to avoid stealing focus.

**Configuration:** `.mcp.json` (project root)
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "playwright": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@playwright/mcp@latest", "--headless"]
    }
  }
}
```

**Benefits:**
- Browser runs invisibly in background
- Does not interrupt user's work
- Screenshots captured without window popup

---

## KISS Philosophy

**KISS is NOT "do the minimum". It IS "don't add complexity that blocks progress".**

The goal is **speed of delivery with quality**. The product must impress the client to win contracts.

### What KISS means (examples)

| ✅ DO                                                         | ❌ DON'T                                                          |
|--------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Add polish, debug tools, UX refinements that accelerate work | Spend days on infrastructure before having something presentable |
| Use best practices that prevent future problems              | Add abstractions for hypothetical future needs                   |
| Preserve knowledge/tips for when they become relevant        | Ignore proven patterns just because "not needed yet"             |

### When to Increase Complexity (examples)

| ✅ Increase complexity when...     | ❌ Do NOT increase when... |
|-----------------------------------|---------------------------|
| Current approach blocks a feature | "It would be cleaner"     |
| Performance is unacceptable       | "Best practices say so"   |
| Code duplication becomes painful  | Premature optimization    |
| Team needs shared abstractions    | Speculative future needs  |

### Example: POC-first approach

When the monorepo setup consumed days debugging config, a pure HTML POC (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) was created to validate the concept quickly.

**Lesson:** Start simple, validate fast, then add complexity incrementally.

---

## Production App: apps/canvas

The production application is located at `apps/canvas/` - a React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6 app that implements the "Semantic Zoom" UX concept.

### Product Entry Point

**`apps/canvas`** - The production React app with semantic zoom.

```bash
# Run development server (http://localhost:5173)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev

# Or from root
bun run dev --filter=@mms/canvas
```

### Tech Stack

| Technology    | Version | Purpose                                   |
|---------------|---------|-------------------------------------------|
| **Vite**      | 5.x     | Build tool and dev server                 |
| **React**     | 18.3.1  | UI framework (required for BlueprintJS 6) |
| **BlueprintJS** | 6.1.0 | UI component library                      |
| **R3F**       | 8.x     | React Three Fiber for WebGL               |
| **drei**      | 9.x     | R3F helpers and components                |
| **Three.js**  | 0.170.x | 3D rendering engine                       |

### Features

| Feature       | Description                                         | LOD Level |
|---------------|-----------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| **Pan**       | Drag to navigate through time (X) and trains (Y)    | All       |
| **Zoom**      | Scroll to zoom in/out, zoom centers on cursor       | All       |
| **Macro View** | Heatmap showing task density (yellow→orange→red)   | < 0.5x    |
| **Meso View** | DNA-like task strips with colored blocks            | 0.5x - 2x |
| **Micro View** | Detailed task cards with labels and grid           | > 2x      |
| **Sidebar**   | Train IDs synced with vertical pan (BlueprintJS Card) | All     |
| **Timeline**  | Date labels with adaptive granularity               | All       |

### Roadmap

See `features.json` for complete feature list. Key phases:

```
✅ F001-F015:  POC validation (archived)
🔄 F101-F103:  Foundation - Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS + R3F ← CURRENT
⏳ F104-F107:  Core rendering - Data, MesoLayer, pan/zoom, LOD
⏳ F108-F110:  All LOD layers - Macro, Micro, transitions
⏳ F111-F114:  UI integration - Sidebar, timeline, controls
⏳ F115-F117:  Polish - Instructions, performance, visual parity
```

---

## POC Reference

A standalone HTML demo exists for visual reference only:

**`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`** - Validated prototype.

```bash
# View the POC reference
open design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html    # macOS
```

Use as visual reference for:
- Appearance of semantic zoom UX
- 3 LOD levels (Macro/Meso/Micro)
- Layout and visual design

---

### Key Rule

**Do NOT write throwaway code without clear time/agility gains.** If code will be rewritten anyway, the simple version must provide faster iteration or validation—otherwise, just build it right the first time.
**If you know something will cause problems later (performance, UX, maintainability), address it now based on knowledge and testing—not fear, not speculation.**

---

## Documentation Rules

- **Language:** Always write documentation in English
- **Location:** Prefer updating existing docs over creating new files
- **Conciseness:** Keep docs actionable, not verbose

---

## Tech Stack

### Production App (apps/canvas)
- **Build Tool:** Vite 5.x
- **Frontend:** React 18.3.1 (required for BlueprintJS 6)
- **UI Components:** BlueprintJS 6.1.0 (Palantir)
- **Graphics Engine:** @react-three/fiber 8.x, @react-three/drei 9.x, three 0.170.x
- **State Management:** Custom state-machine (packages/toolkit)

### Monorepo Infrastructure
- **Runtime:** Bun 1.2.0
- **Monorepo:** Turborepo
- **Shared Package:** @mms/toolkit (React 18 || 19 compatible)

## Quick Commands

> ⚠️ **ALWAYS run `./init.sh --no-server` first** to verify environment.

### Production App (apps/canvas)

```bash
# 1. Initialize and verify environment
./init.sh --no-server

# 2. Install dependencies
bun i

# 3. Run production app (http://localhost:5173)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev

# 4. Build for production
cd apps/canvas && bun run build

# 5. Type check
bun run typecheck
```

### POC Reference

```bash
# View the POC (reference only)
open design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html
```

### Troubleshooting

This is a **monorepo**. If root scripts fail, run directly in the app:

```bash
# Direct command (always works)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev

# Check what scripts exist
cat apps/canvas/package.json | grep -A10 '"scripts"'
```

---

## CRITICAL AI RULES

> **READ THIS FIRST.** These rules address fundamental AI limitations.

### Rule 1: CHECK THE CURRENT DATE

**Your training data is outdated.** Before starting ANY work:

```bash
date  # Check actual current date
```

**DO NOT assume the current year is your training year.** If your training was in 2024 but it's now 2025:
- Documentation has changed
- Library versions have changed
- APIs have changed
- Best practices have evolved

**Always include the CURRENT YEAR in documentation searches.**

### Rule 2: YOU CANNOT SEE THE UI

**You are blind to rendered output.** You MUST use MCP visual testing.

```
What you know:              Reality:
✓ Code compiled             ✗ Blank white screen
✓ No TypeScript errors      ✗ React error boundary
✓ Tests passed              ✗ Component doesn't render
```

**NEVER mark a UI feature as complete without MCP screenshot verification.**

### Rule 3: INSTALL DEPENDENCIES VIA COMMANDS

**NEVER manually edit dependency files to add packages.**

```bash
# CORRECT
bun add react
bun add @react-three/fiber

# WRONG - DO NOT DO THIS
# Manually editing package.json: "react": "^18.0.0"
```

Why: Package managers resolve correct versions and peer dependencies.

### Rule 4: RESEARCH BEFORE GUESSING

**When stuck, STOP and research.** Don't try random solutions.

See the STOP-SEARCH-UNDERSTAND-APPLY method below.

### Rule 5: SEARCH FOR UPDATED DOCUMENTATION

**Your training data is stale.** For complex configurations:

```bash
# Always search with current year
WebSearch: "[technology] [topic] documentation 2025"
WebFetch: [official docs URL]
```

**Do NOT rely on training data for:**
- Library configurations
- API patterns
- Framework setup
- Plugin systems

### Rule 6: VERIFY COMMANDS BEFORE RUNNING

**NEVER assume a command will work.** Before running ANY command:

```bash
# WRONG - Running blindly
bun run dev  # ← May fail if monorepo filter is wrong

# CORRECT - Verify first
cat package.json | grep -A10 '"scripts"'  # Check what scripts exist
./init.sh --no-server                      # Verify environment
bun run dev                                 # Then run
```

**If a command fails:**
1. **READ the error message** - Don't retry blindly
2. **Check the configuration** - Is the command correct for this project?
3. **Try the direct approach** - `cd apps/canvas && bun run dev`
4. **Document the solution** - Update relevant docs so future sessions don't repeat the error

**Common monorepo gotchas:**
- Root `package.json` scripts may use filters that don't match package names
- Always check `apps/*/package.json` for the actual package name
- When in doubt, run commands directly in the package folder

---

## MCP VISUAL TESTING (MANDATORY)

### Why This is Required

You cannot see the app you're building. Without visual testing, you will:
- Mark blank screens as "working"
- Miss CSS/layout issues
- Overlook runtime errors visible only in UI

### Testing Protocol: Playwright MCP

Use **Playwright MCP** for visual tests. Runs headless and **does not steal focus** from the user's window.

#### Step 1: Navigate to the page

```
mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="file:///full/path/to/file.html"
```

Or for local server:
```
mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="http://localhost:5173"
```

#### Step 2: Check console (JS errors)

```
mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages level="error"
```

- Red errors → **FAIL** (investigate before continuing)
- Yellow warnings → Evaluate (e.g., "Tailwind CDN production warning" is OK)

#### Step 3: Capture screenshot

```
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot filename="screenshots/test-name.png"
```

For scrollable pages:
```
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot fullPage=true filename="screenshots/test-full.png"
```

#### Step 4: Analyze visually

Check in the screenshot:
- [ ] Content visible? (not blank/empty)
- [ ] Layout correct? (elements positioned as expected)
- [ ] Colors correct? (matching design)
- [ ] No visual artifacts?

#### Step 5: Interactions (if needed)

```
mcp__playwright__browser_click element="button X" ref="e123"
mcp__playwright__browser_type element="field Y" ref="e456" text="value"
```

#### Step 6: Close browser at the end

```
mcp__playwright__browser_close
```

### Approval Criteria

| Criterion | PASS | FAIL |
|-----------|------|------|
| Console without errors | ✅ | ❌ Red errors |
| Content rendered | ✅ | ❌ Blank/empty screen |
| Layout correct | ✅ | ❌ Misaligned elements |
| JS executed | ✅ | ❌ Dynamic content missing |

### Final Decision

```
Visual OK + Console clean    → passes: true
Visual OK + Console warnings → Evaluate (may pass)
Visual OK + Console errors   → FAIL (fix it)
Visual broken                → FAIL (fix it)
```

### Playwright MCP Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` | Open page (file:// or http://) |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot` | Capture viewport or fullPage |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` | Accessibility tree (debug) |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages` | View JS errors/warnings |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_click` | Click on element |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_type` | Type in field |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_close` | Close browser |

## ERROR HANDLING: STOP-SEARCH-UNDERSTAND-APPLY

### Classify the Error First

|------------|--------|
| Typo, syntax, obvious | Fix directly |

```
1. STOP

2. SEARCH
   - WebSearch: "[error] + [tech] + [CURRENT YEAR]"
   - WebFetch: Official documentation
   - Include current year, not training year

3. UNDERSTAND
   - Read results carefully
   - Identify ROOT CAUSE
   - Know WHY before fixing

4. APPLY
   - Implement correct solution
   - From documentation, not guessing

5. DOCUMENT
   - Update relevant docs if this is a recurring issue
```

## LOD System (Level of Detail)

The system implements three rendering layers based on camera zoom:

- **Macro (zoom < 0.5):** Procedural shader heatmap - 1 draw call
- **Meso (0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0):** InstancedMesh with 60k instances
- **Micro (zoom > 2.0):** HTML overlay with Line2 dependency curves

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)

## Project Overview
High-Performance Enterprise Integrated Planning System with "Semantic Zoom" UX (Google Earth-style) for metro fleet management. Renders 50+ trains and 10 years of tasks at constant 60fps using WebGL.

---

## HARNESS: Fundamental Concept (READ THIS FIRST)

> **This project uses a Harness.** If you don't know what that is, STOP and research:
> - [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

### What is a Harness?

A **Harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.

**Problem:** Each new Claude Code session starts with NO memory of previous sessions.

**Solution:** State files persist context between sessions:
- `claude-progress.txt` → session history
- `features.json` → requirements with status (passes: true/false)

### Work Protocol

```
1. READ claude-progress.txt (understand current state)
2. READ features.json (find next feature)
3. IMPLEMENT one feature
4. TEST visually (Playwright MCP)
5. COMMIT after passing
6. UPDATE claude-progress.txt
7. STOP (leave codebase production-ready)
```

### Self-Correction Protocol

If you don't understand what harness is after reading this:
1. **SEARCH:** WebSearch "Anthropic harness agents 2025"
2. **ANALYZE:** Why was local documentation unclear?
3. **FIX:** Update CLAUDE.md or hooks to improve clarity
4. **DOCUMENT:** Record the fix in claude-progress.txt

### Harness Files

| File                  | Purpose              | When to Read       |
|-----------------------|----------------------|--------------------|
| `claude-progress.txt` | Session history      | **ALWAYS first**   |
| `features.json`       | Features with status | After progress.txt |
| `CLAUDE.md`           | Project rules        | Reference          |

---

## Security & Permissions

### File Access Restrictions

This project uses **deny permissions** to prevent access to personal directories outside the project.

**Protected directories** (access denied):
- `~/Desktop`, `~/Documents`, `~/Downloads`
- `~/Pictures`, `~/Movies`, `~/Music`
- Any `find ~` or `find /Users` commands

**Configuration:** `.claude/settings.local.json` contains deny rules that block:
- Read/Write/Edit/Glob/Grep operations on protected paths
- Bash commands that scan home directories

### Playwright MCP Configuration

Visual testing uses Playwright MCP in **headless mode** to avoid stealing focus.

**Configuration:** `.mcp.json` (project root)
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "playwright": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@playwright/mcp@latest", "--headless"]
    }
  }
}
```

**Benefits:**
- Browser runs invisibly in background
- Does not interrupt user's work
- Screenshots captured without window popup

---

## KISS Philosophy

**KISS is NOT "do the minimum". It IS "don't add complexity that blocks progress".**

The goal is **speed of delivery with quality**. The product must impress the client to win contracts.

### What KISS means (examples)

| ✅ DO                                                         | ❌ DON'T                                                          |
|--------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Add polish, debug tools, UX refinements that accelerate work | Spend days on infrastructure before having something presentable |
| Use best practices that prevent future problems              | Add abstractions for hypothetical future needs                   |
| Preserve knowledge/tips for when they become relevant        | Ignore proven patterns just because "not needed yet"             |

### When to Increase Complexity (examples)

| ✅ Increase complexity when...     | ❌ Do NOT increase when... |
|-----------------------------------|---------------------------|
| Current approach blocks a feature | "It would be cleaner"     |
| Performance is unacceptable       | "Best practices say so"   |
| Code duplication becomes painful  | Premature optimization    |
| Team needs shared abstractions    | Speculative future needs  |

### Example: POC-first approach

When the monorepo setup consumed days debugging config, a pure HTML POC (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) was created to validate the concept quickly.

**Lesson:** Start simple, validate fast, then add complexity incrementally.

---

## Production App: apps/canvas

The production application is located at `apps/canvas/` - a React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6 app that implements the "Semantic Zoom" UX concept.

### Product Entry Point

**`apps/canvas`** - The production React app with semantic zoom.

```bash
# Run development server (http://localhost:5173)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev

# Or from root
bun run dev --filter=@mms/canvas
```

### Tech Stack

| Technology    | Version | Purpose                                   |
|---------------|---------|-------------------------------------------|
| **Vite**      | 5.x     | Build tool and dev server                 |
| **React**     | 18.3.1  | UI framework (required for BlueprintJS 6) |
| **BlueprintJS** | 6.1.0 | UI component library                      |
| **R3F**       | 8.x     | React Three Fiber for WebGL               |
| **drei**      | 9.x     | R3F helpers and components                |
| **Three.js**  | 0.170.x | 3D rendering engine                       |

### Features

| Feature       | Description                                         | LOD Level |
|---------------|-----------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| **Pan**       | Drag to navigate through time (X) and trains (Y)    | All       |
| **Zoom**      | Scroll to zoom in/out, zoom centers on cursor       | All       |
| **Macro View** | Heatmap showing task density (yellow→orange→red)   | < 0.5x    |
| **Meso View** | DNA-like task strips with colored blocks            | 0.5x - 2x |
| **Micro View** | Detailed task cards with labels and grid           | > 2x      |
| **Sidebar**   | Train IDs synced with vertical pan (BlueprintJS Card) | All     |
| **Timeline**  | Date labels with adaptive granularity               | All       |

### Roadmap

See `features.json` for complete feature list. Key phases:

```
✅ F001-F015:  POC validation (archived)
🔄 F101-F103:  Foundation - Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS + R3F ← CURRENT
⏳ F104-F107:  Core rendering - Data, MesoLayer, pan/zoom, LOD
⏳ F108-F110:  All LOD layers - Macro, Micro, transitions
⏳ F111-F114:  UI integration - Sidebar, timeline, controls
⏳ F115-F116:  Polish - Instructions, performance
```

---

## POC Reference

A standalone HTML demo exists for visual reference only:

**`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`** - Validated prototype.

```bash
# View the POC reference
open design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html    # macOS
```

Use as visual reference for:
- Appearance of semantic zoom UX
- 3 LOD levels (Macro/Meso/Micro)
- Layout and visual design

---

### Key Rule

**Do NOT write throwaway code without clear time/agility gains.** If code will be rewritten anyway, the simple version must provide faster iteration or validation—otherwise, just build it right the first time.
**If you know something will cause problems later (performance, UX, maintainability), address it now based on knowledge and testing—not fear, not speculation.**

---

## Documentation Rules

- **Language:** Always write documentation in English
- **Location:** Prefer updating existing docs over creating new files
- **Conciseness:** Keep docs actionable, not verbose

---

## Tech Stack

### Production App (apps/canvas)
- **Build Tool:** Vite 5.x
- **Frontend:** React 18.3.1 (required for BlueprintJS 6)
- **UI Components:** BlueprintJS 6.1.0 (Palantir)
- **Graphics Engine:** @react-three/fiber 8.x, @react-three/drei 9.x, three 0.170.x
- **State Management:** Custom state-machine (packages/toolkit)

### Monorepo Infrastructure
- **Runtime:** Bun 1.2.0
- **Monorepo:** Turborepo
- **Shared Package:** @mms/toolkit (React 18 || 19 compatible)

## Quick Commands

> ⚠️ **ALWAYS run `./init.sh --no-server` first** to verify environment.

### Production App (apps/canvas)

```bash
# 1. Initialize and verify environment
./init.sh --no-server

# 2. Install dependencies
bun i

# 3. Run production app (http://localhost:5173)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev

# 4. Build for production
cd apps/canvas && bun run build

# 5. Type check
bun run typecheck
```

```bash
# Direct command (always works)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev

# Check what scripts exist
cat apps/canvas/package.json | grep -A10 '"scripts"'
```

├── docs/                 # Domain documentation
├── CLAUDE.md             # This file (project rules)
├── claude-progress.txt   # Session history
├── features.json         # Feature requirements (source of truth)
└── agent_docs/           # Architecture, testing, conventions
```

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `apps/canvas/src/main.tsx` | Production app entry |
| `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` | Main React component |
| `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` | R3F canvas |
| `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/*.tsx` | LOD rendering layers |
| `apps/canvas/src/data/generator.ts` | Mock data generation |
| `packages/toolkit/` | Shared state machine |
| `design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html` | POC (visual reference only) |

## LOD System (Level of Detail)

The system implements three rendering layers based on camera zoom:

- **Macro (zoom < 0.5):** Procedural shader heatmap - 1 draw call
- **Meso (0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0):** InstancedMesh with 60k instances
- **Micro (zoom > 2.0):** HTML overlay with Line2 dependency curves

## Current State

Read `claude-progress.txt` for session history and `features.json` for requirements status.

## Documentation

### Domain Documentation (`docs/`)
- @docs/README.md - Documentation index
- @docs/DOMAIN.md - Fleet, maintenance types, entities
- @docs/REQUIREMENTS.md - Business requirements (from RFQ)
- @docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md - UX design and interactions
- ./docs/RESEARCH.md - Industry research and best practices for systems like this.

### Technical Documentation (`agent_docs/`)
- @agent_docs/architecture.md - Architecture details
- @agent_docs/testing.md - Testing protocol
- @agent_docs/conventions.md - Code conventions
- @agent_docs/ai-limitations.md - AI limitations and mitigations

lines: ⏳ F104-F107:  Core rendering - Data, MesoLayer, pan/zoom, LOD
⏳ F108-F110:  All LOD layers - Macro, Micro, transitions
⏳ F111-F114:  UI integration - Sidebar, timeline, controls
+⏳ F115-F117:  Polish - Instructions, performance, visual parity

# MMS System Documentation

This directory contains domain knowledge, requirements, research, and UX specifications for the Metro Integrated Planning System.

## Document Index

| Document | Description | Audience |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| [DOMAIN.md](./DOMAIN.md) | Fleet composition, maintenance types, domain entities | Developers, Domain Experts |
| [REQUIREMENTS.md](./REQUIREMENTS.md) | Business requirements (from RFQ) | Product, Engineering |
| [UX_SPECIFICATION.md](./UX_SPECIFICATION.md) | UX design and interaction patterns | Designers, Frontend Devs |
| [UX_RESEARCH.md](./UX_RESEARCH.md) | Pan/zoom UX research (Figma, Maps, iOS patterns) | Frontend Devs |
| [RESEARCH.md](./RESEARCH.md) | Industry research on maintenance scheduling | All stakeholders |
| [QA_PROTOCOL.md](./QA_PROTOCOL.md) | Mandatory QA testing protocol for canvas/WebGL | AI Agents, QA |

## Project Context

### What is MMS?

The **Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)** is a high-performance visualization tool for railway rolling stock maintenance planning. It provides a "Single Pane of Glass" solution that unifies:

- **Strategic Planning** (1-12 years): Budget allocation, major overhauls
- **Tactical Planning** (weeks-months): Workload balancing, resource leveling
- **Operational Planning** (daily): Run assignments, track allocation

### The "Semantic Zoom" Concept

Inspired by Google Earth, the system implements **semantic zoom** - the visualization automatically transforms based on zoom level:

| Zoom Level | Threshold | Time Scale | Visualization |
|------------|-----------|------------|---------------|
| Macro | < 0.5x | 10-20 years | Heatmap (yellow→orange→red) |
| Meso | 0.5-2x | 2-5 years | Task blocks (DNA pattern) |
| Micro | > 2x | 4-8 weeks | Detailed cards with labels |

### Current Implementation

**Production:** `apps/canvas` - React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6 app with semantic zoom.

```bash
# Run production app (http://localhost:5173)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev
```

**Production Features (F101-F116):**

| Phase | Features | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| **Foundation** | F101-F103 | Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS + R3F setup |
| **Core Rendering** | F104-F107 | Data, MesoLayer, pan/zoom, LOD state |
| **All Layers** | F108-F110 | MacroLayer, MicroLayer, smooth transitions |
| **UI Integration** | F111-F114 | Sidebar, timeline, controls, conflicts |
| **Polish** | F115-F116 | Instructions, performance |

**POC Reference:**

The POC (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) serves as visual reference only:
- Visual appearance of semantic zoom UX
- 3 LOD levels (Macro/Meso/Micro)
- Sidebar and timeline layout
- Conflict visual indicators

See `features.json` for complete feature list and status.

## Relationship to Implementation

### Production App (`apps/canvas`)

The UX patterns in [UX_SPECIFICATION.md](./UX_SPECIFICATION.md) are implemented in:
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/` - R3F rendering components
- `apps/canvas/src/components/` - BlueprintJS UI components
- `apps/canvas/src/hooks/` - State management (useViewport)

### Domain Entities

The domain entities in [DOMAIN.md](./DOMAIN.md) are implemented in:
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - TypeScript interfaces
- `apps/canvas/src/data/generator.ts` - Mock data generation

### POC Reference

The validated prototype (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) serves as visual reference only:
- Demonstrates semantic zoom concept visually
- Visual reference for production implementation
- Shows what the 3 LOD levels should look like

### Future Development

The full RFQ requirements in [REQUIREMENTS.md](./REQUIREMENTS.md) include features not yet implemented:
- Real-time mileage data integration
- What-If scenario simulation
- Cost forecasting and budget tracking
- IBM Maximo integration

These represent the product roadmap beyond the current demo.

## Quick Links

- **Technical Architecture**: See `agent_docs/architecture.md`
- **Visual References**: See `design/README.md`
- **Code Conventions**: See `agent_docs/conventions.md`
- **Session Progress**: See `claude-progress.txt`

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "steps": [
        "Research Playwright headless configuration options",
        "Update MCP settings for headless mode",
        "Test that browser windows don't steal focus during visual tests",
        "Document configuration in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Playwright MCP configured with --headless flag in .mcp.json. Deny permissions added to block access to personal directories."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking enabled",
        "Research QA best practices for canvas/WebGL applications",
        "Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests",
        "Define QA checklist based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries",
        "Update harness hooks to enforce QA step",
        "Document in docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md with WebGL testing protocol. Updated session-start.sh and end-of-turn-check.sh hooks. Protocol includes: typecheck, visual test, console check, interaction test, performance check sequence."
    },
    {
      "id": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "category": "research",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking",
        "Study UI patterns: Canva, Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Android",
        "Document all mouse events (click, dblclick, drag, hover, wheel, context)",
        "Document all touch events (tap, pinch, swipe, long-press)",
        "Research cross-platform differences (macOS/Windows/Linux scroll)",
        "Research hardware differences (trackpad vs mouse)",
        "Create docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with all findings",
        "Update harness to preserve context across sessions"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with comprehensive research on: Figma/Google Maps/iOS patterns, mouse/touch/pointer events, wheel normalization (Facebook pattern), momentum physics, cross-platform scroll differences, accessibility requirements. Includes implementation recommendations for F118-F121."
    },
    {
      "id": "F118",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Momentum/inertia panning (velocity-based drag)",
      "steps": [
        "Track drag velocity during mouse movement",
        "On mouse release, continue panning with velocity",
        "Apply friction to gradually stop",
        "Test on macOS, Windows (if available)",
        "Match Google Maps/iOS feel"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with velocity tracking during drag, friction-based animation loop (0.93 friction), and momentum stops at velocity < 0.5. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F119",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Smooth zoom UX (iOS/Google Maps feel)",
      "steps": [
        "Add easing to zoom transitions",
        "Implement zoom momentum",
        "Add pinch-to-zoom support for touch devices",
        "Smooth out zoom steps",
        "Test across different input devices"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented quadOut easing for zoom animations (250ms), wheel event accumulation for trackpad smoothing, pinch-to-zoom support via Pointer Events API, animated zoom for +/- buttons and Reset. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F120",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Double-click to zoom in (BUG FIX)",
      "steps": [
        "Add dblclick event handler to canvas",
        "Calculate click position in world coordinates",
        "Zoom 2x centered on click position",
        "Animate zoom transition smoothly",
        "Respect MAX_ZOOM limit"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with 350ms cubicOut animation. Zooms 2x toward click point, respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x). Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F121",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Cross-platform scroll/input sensitivity normalization",
      "steps": [
        "Research deltaY values across browsers/OS",
        "Normalize scroll sensitivity for macOS/Windows/Linux",
        "Handle trackpad vs mouse differences",
        "Implement adaptive sensitivity based on input type",
        "Test on multiple platforms"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented deltaMode normalization (LINE/PAGE to pixels), trackpad vs mouse detection by event frequency, adaptive sensitivity (mouse: 0.003, trackpad: 0.001), zoom delta clamping (±0.5 per event). Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F122",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "BUG FIX: Elastic/jump effect when dragging canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Investigate elastic/jump behavior during drag",
        "Check momentum calculation and velocity samples",
        "Review camera position clamping logic",
        "Test on slow and fast drags",
        "Ensure smooth continuous pan without jumps"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Root cause: React state batching caused stale cameraPos when starting new drag during momentum. Fix: Added cameraPosRef for synchronous position tracking. handlePointerDown now uses ref instead of state. All setCameraPos calls sync to ref via updateCameraPos(). Tested with rapid drag interruptions via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F116",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 3,
      "description": "Performance optimization (60fps)",
      "steps": [
        "Profile with Chrome DevTools",
        "Maintain 60fps during pan/zoom",
        "No memory leaks on extended use",
        "InstancedMesh optimizations",
        "Frustum culling for off-screen tasks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F110",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Optimized MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh. All layers now use InstancedMesh for optimal draw calls. MicroLayer already had frustum culling and optimized rendering. Visual tests pass at all LOD levels (MACRO/MESO/MICRO). No console errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "F123",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Minimap navigation (bird's eye view)",
      "steps": [
        "Research minimap patterns in design tools (Figma, Canva) and games",
        "Create minimap component showing entire canvas at small scale",
        "Show viewport rectangle indicating current view position",
        "Allow click/drag on minimap to navigate",
        "Position in corner (bottom-right recommended)",
        "Add toggle to show/hide minimap"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "F110",
      "notes": "FEATURE REQUEST: Add minimap like games/design tools to help users locate themselves in the larger canvas. Shows entire 10-year timeline + 50 trains at small scale with viewport indicator."
    },
    {
      "id": "TECH-DEBT-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Migrate camera state to @mms/toolkit state-machine (replace React hooks)",
      "steps": [
        "✅ Research: @mms/toolkit already uses useSyncExternalStore (ideal for WebGL)",
        "✅ Research: Store.create() provides external store with subscribe/getState/dispatch",
        "✅ Research: useMachine() hook returns [state, actions] tuple",
        "Create viewport slice in @mms/toolkit for camera state (zoom, panX, panY, lod)",
        "Create canvas machine with viewport slice + future slices (selection, hover)",
        "Migrate InfiniteCanvas from useState/useRef to useMachine('viewport')",
        "Remove cameraPosRef workaround (external store solves stale closure issue)",
        "Test: rapid drag interruptions, momentum, zoom animations"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "IN PROGRESS: @mms/toolkit already has the ideal pattern (useSyncExternalStore). No need for Zustand/Jotai. Just need to create viewport slice and migrate InfiniteCanvas."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-002",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Reduce harness file sizes (archive completed features)",
      "steps": [
        "Analyze features.json size and identify bloat",
        "Move ALL completed features (passes:true) to separate archive file",
        "Keep only pending features in main features.json",
        "Update harness hooks to read from both files if needed",
        "Ensure context is preserved for AI agents"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "features.json is getting large with completed features that may not add value to current context."
    },
    {
      "id": "F124",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Sidebar and Timeline interactive navigation",
      "steps": [
        "Research best UX patterns for scrollable sidebars (Figma, VS Code, design tools)",
        "Add clickable train IDs in sidebar to navigate directly to that train",
        "Add clickable dates in timeline header to navigate to that time",
        "Implement search/filter functionality to jump to specific train",
        "Add keyboard shortcut for quick navigation (Cmd+G/Ctrl+G 'Go to...')",
        "Consider dropdown or autocomplete for direct navigation",
        "Ensure smooth animated transition when navigating"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented clickable train IDs in sidebar and clickable date labels in timeline. Navigation uses smooth 350ms animation. Hover effects with background highlight. Tooltips show 'Click to navigate to...'. Search/filter and keyboard shortcuts deferred to future enhancement. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Canvas renders and initializes without errors",
      "steps": [
        "Open design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html in browser",
        "Canvas displays with header, sidebar, and main area",
        "No JavaScript errors in console",
        "Mock data generates 50 trains with 10 years of tasks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "POC validated semantic zoom concept. Production app: apps/canvas"
    },
    {
      "id": "F002",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Pan navigation with drag",
      "steps": [
        "Click and drag on canvas to pan",
        "Cursor changes to grabbing while dragging",
        "Pan works in all directions (X and Y)",
        "Sidebar and timeline header sync with pan position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Implemented with mousedown/mousemove/mouseup events"
    },
    {
      "id": "F003",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom navigation with scroll wheel",
      "steps": [
        "Scroll up to zoom in, scroll down to zoom out",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor position",
        "Zoom respects MIN_ZOOM (0.1x) and MAX_ZOOM (5x) limits",
        "Zoom level indicator updates in header"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Zoom toward mouse position for natural feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F004",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Double-click zoom",
      "steps": [
        "Double-click zooms in 2x at click location",
        "Works at any current zoom level (respects max)",
        "Animation is smooth (no jarring jump)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Quick zoom in gesture"
    },
    {
      "id": "F005",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom button controls",
      "steps": [
        "Plus button zooms in by 1.5x",
        "Minus button zooms out by 1.5x",
        "Reset button returns to zoom 1.0 and pan origin",
        "Buttons zoom toward center of viewport"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Alternative for users without scroll wheel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F006",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Macro layer renders at zoom < 0.5x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom out below 0.5x to trigger Macro view",
        "Heatmap displays with Yellow-Orange-Red gradient",
        "Cell density reflects task count per month/train",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MACRO' with yellow badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Strategic 10-20 year overview"
    },
    {
      "id": "F007",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Meso layer renders at zoom 0.5x-2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom to 0.5x-2x range to trigger Meso view",
        "Task blocks render as colored rectangles (DNA strips)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MESO' with blue badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Tactical 2-5 year view with task blocks"
    },
    {
      "id": "F008",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Micro layer renders at zoom > 2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom above 2x to trigger Micro view",
        "Task cards render with shadows and rounded corners",
        "Grid lines show days (vertical) and trains (horizontal)",
        "Task type labels visible when card is wide enough",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MICRO' with green badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Operational 4-8 week view with detailed cards"
    },
    {
      "id": "F009",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "LOD transitions are smooth",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom from Macro to Meso - no flickering",
        "Zoom from Meso to Micro - no flickering",
        "State persists correctly across transitions",
        "Performance stays smooth during transition"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F006",
      "notes": "Critical for Google Earth-like UX feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F010",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Sidebar displays train IDs synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Train IDs visible in left sidebar",
        "IDs update as canvas pans vertically",
        "Row height matches canvas zoom level",
        "Only visible trains are rendered (virtual scroll)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Sidebar syncs with panY position"
    },
    {
      "id": "F011",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Timeline header displays dates synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Timeline labels update as canvas pans horizontally",
        "Granularity changes based on zoom (years/months/weeks/days)",
        "Labels are readable and don't overlap",
        "Timeline syncs with panX position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Adaptive granularity: years at low zoom, days at high zoom"
    },
    {
      "id": "F012",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Conflict indicators visible on tasks",
      "steps": [
        "Tasks with conflicts show red border/glow",
        "Conflicts visible in Meso layer (red stroke)",
        "Conflicts visible in Micro layer (red glow shadow)",
        "~5% of tasks have conflict flag for testing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Red highlight draws attention to scheduling issues"
    },
    {
      "id": "F013",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Task colors match design specifications",
      "steps": [
        "Maintenance tasks: Blue gradient (#3b82f6 to #2563eb)",
        "Overhaul tasks: Orange gradient (#f59e0b to #d97706)",
        "Inspection tasks: Gray gradient (#9ca3af to #6b7280)",
        "Gradients render correctly at all zoom levels"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Color coding matches docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md"
    },
    {
      "id": "F014",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "Onboarding instructions display and auto-hide",
      "steps": [
        "Instructions tooltip visible on page load",
        "Shows: 'Scroll to zoom - Drag to pan - Double-click to zoom in'",
        "Auto-fades after 5 seconds",
        "Non-intrusive (pointer-events: none)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Progressive disclosure - teach on first use"
    },
    {
      "id": "F015",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "LOD legend explains zoom levels",
      "steps": [
        "Legend panel visible in bottom-left corner",
        "Shows Macro (yellow), Meso (blue), Micro (green) colors",
        "Describes zoom thresholds (<0.5x, 0.5-2x, >2x)",
        "Helps users understand semantic zoom concept"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Educational UI element"
    },
    {
      "id": "F101",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Create Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS app skeleton",
      "steps": [
        "Create apps/canvas directory with Vite config",
        "Install React 18.3.1, BlueprintJS 6.1.0",
        "Create basic App.tsx with BlueprintJS Navbar",
        "App runs on localhost:5173 without errors",
        "BlueprintJS styles load correctly"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "Foundation for production app - React 18 required for BlueprintJS 6"
    },
    {
      "id": "F102",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Add R3F Canvas with OrthographicCamera",
      "steps": [
        "Install @react-three/fiber and @react-three/drei",
        "Create InfiniteCanvas component with R3F Canvas",
        "Setup OrthographicCamera for 2D-like view",
        "Canvas fills available space below navbar",
        "No WebGL errors in console"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "R3F 8.x compatible with React 18. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F103",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Basic BlueprintJS layout (Navbar, sidebar placeholder)",
      "steps": [
        "Navbar with app title and zoom indicator",
        "Left sidebar container for train IDs",
        "Main area for R3F canvas",
        "Layout uses flexbox, fills viewport",
        "Responsive to window resize"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "BlueprintJS Card, Navbar, ButtonGroup. Sidebar syncs with canvas panY. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F104",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Data types and mock generator",
      "steps": [
        "Create Train, Task, Config TypeScript interfaces",
        "Implement generateData() function",
        "Generate 50 trains with 10 years of tasks",
        "~5% tasks have conflict flag",
        "Data available via React context or store"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "types.ts + generator.ts in apps/canvas/src/data/. Persistent types use ISO strings, render types use numeric offsets."
    },
    {
      "id": "F105",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MesoLayer - InstancedMesh task blocks",
      "steps": [
        "Create MesoLayer component using InstancedMesh",
        "Render task blocks as colored rectangles",
        "Colors: blue (maintenance), orange (overhaul), gray (inspection)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "Tasks visible at default zoom (1.0x)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F104",
      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh. Fixed architectural issue: camera init now uses R3F size (not container div). Tasks aligned with sidebar. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F106",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Pan and Zoom camera controls",
      "steps": [
        "Drag to pan (mousedown + mousemove)",
        "Scroll to zoom (wheel event)",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor",
        "Respect MIN_ZOOM (0.1) and MAX_ZOOM (5.0)",
        "Cursor changes to grab/grabbing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "Pan bounded to world limits. Zoom via wheel and buttons. Camera initialized using R3F size (not container div). Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F107",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "LOD state management",
      "steps": [
        "Create useViewport hook with zoom, panX, panY state",
        "Compute LOD from zoom: MACRO (<0.5), MESO (0.5-2), MICRO (>2)",
        "LOD changes trigger layer visibility",
        "State persists across renders",
        "Export state for UI components"
      ],

Includes implementation recommendations for F118-F121."

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# MMS System Documentation

This directory contains domain knowledge, requirements, research, and UX specifications for the Metro Integrated Planning System.

## Document Index

| Document | Description | Audience |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| [DOMAIN.md](./DOMAIN.md) | Fleet composition, maintenance types, domain entities | Developers, Domain Experts |
| [REQUIREMENTS.md](./REQUIREMENTS.md) | Business requirements (from RFQ) | Product, Engineering |
| [UX_SPECIFICATION.md](./UX_SPECIFICATION.md) | UX design and interaction patterns | Designers, Frontend Devs |
| [UX_RESEARCH.md](./UX_RESEARCH.md) | Pan/zoom UX research (Figma, Maps, iOS patterns) | Frontend Devs |
| [RESEARCH.md](./RESEARCH.md) | Industry research on maintenance scheduling | All stakeholders |
| [QA_PROTOCOL.md](./QA_PROTOCOL.md) | Mandatory QA testing protocol for canvas/WebGL | AI Agents, QA |

## Project Context

### What is MMS?

The **Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)** is a high-performance visualization tool for railway rolling stock maintenance planning. It provides a "Single Pane of Glass" solution that unifies:

- **Strategic Planning** (1-12 years): Budget allocation, major overhauls
- **Tactical Planning** (weeks-months): Workload balancing, resource leveling
- **Operational Planning** (daily): Run assignments, track allocation

### The "Semantic Zoom" Concept

Inspired by Google Earth, the system implements **semantic zoom** - the visualization automatically transforms based on zoom level:

| Zoom Level | Threshold | Time Scale | Visualization |
|------------|-----------|------------|---------------|
| Macro | < 0.5x | 10-20 years | Heatmap (yellow→orange→red) |
| Meso | 0.5-2x | 2-5 years | Task blocks (DNA pattern) |
| Micro | > 2x | 4-8 weeks | Detailed cards with labels |

### Current Implementation

**Production:** `apps/canvas` - React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6 app with semantic zoom.

```bash
# Run production app (http://localhost:5173)
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev
```

**Production Features (F101-F116):**

| Phase | Features | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| **Foundation** | F101-F103 | Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS + R3F setup |
| **Core Rendering** | F104-F107 | Data, MesoLayer, pan/zoom, LOD state |
| **All Layers** | F108-F110 | MacroLayer, MicroLayer, smooth transitions |
| **UI Integration** | F111-F114 | Sidebar, timeline, controls, conflicts |
| **Polish** | F115-F116 | Instructions, performance |

**POC Reference:**

The POC (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) serves as visual reference only:
- Visual appearance of semantic zoom UX
- 3 LOD levels (Macro/Meso/Micro)
- Sidebar and timeline layout
- Conflict visual indicators

See `features.json` for complete feature list and status.

## Relationship to Implementation

### Production App (`apps/canvas`)

The UX patterns in [UX_SPECIFICATION.md](./UX_SPECIFICATION.md) are implemented in:
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/` - R3F rendering components
- `apps/canvas/src/components/` - BlueprintJS UI components
- `apps/canvas/src/hooks/` - State management (useViewport)

### Domain Entities

The domain entities in [DOMAIN.md](./DOMAIN.md) are implemented in:
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - TypeScript interfaces
- `apps/canvas/src/data/generator.ts` - Mock data generation

### POC Reference

The validated prototype (`design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html`) serves as visual reference only:
- Demonstrates semantic zoom concept visually
- Visual reference for production implementation
- Shows what the 3 LOD levels should look like

### Future Development

The full RFQ requirements in [REQUIREMENTS.md](./REQUIREMENTS.md) include features not yet implemented:
- Real-time mileage data integration
- What-If scenario simulation
- Cost forecasting and budget tracking
- IBM Maximo integration

These represent the product roadmap beyond the current demo.

## Quick Links

- **Technical Architecture**: See `agent_docs/architecture.md`
- **Visual References**: See `design/README.md`
- **Code Conventions**: See `agent_docs/conventions.md`
- **Session Progress**: See `claude-progress.txt`
